results.rendered_html:
1. upā-√vft Ā. (rarely P.) -vartate, -ti (Impv. 2. pl. -vavṛdhvam [for -vavṛddhvam]  RV. ) to turn towards, go towards, approach, stand by the side of  RV. viii, 20, 18 ;  TS. ;  TBr. ;  ŚBr. ;  AitBr. ;  MBh.  &c.;
to apply one's self to, approach for protection,  MBh. ;  BhP. ;
to fall to one's share,  MBh. ;  BhP. ;
to return, come back,  MBh. ;  R. ;  Śak.  &c.:
Caus. P. -vartayati, to cause to turn or go towards,  ŚBr. ;  KātyŚr. ;
to lead near or back, bring back,  MBh. ;  R. ;  Daś. ;
to draw back, divert, cause to desist from,  Suśr. ;  MBh. ;
to cause to get breath (e.g. horses),  MBh. vii.

results.rendered_html:
upāvṛt 1 A.
1 To turn or go towards, approach.
2 To turn away from, return.
3 To give.
— Caus.
1 To win over, induce;  Māl. 8. 
2 To cause to turn; lead or bring back;  M. 5. 
3 To give to.
4 To cause, produce.
5 To stretch out more and more.
